2605.25746.title: Multi-Agent Coordination Adaptation via Structure-Guided Orchestration
2605.25746.paper_summary: As large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems scale to handle increasingly complex tasks, balancing structural stability and dynamic adaptability becomes increasingly challenging. Existing systems typically adopt either structure-centric methods, committing to structures determined upfront that limit fine-grained control, or orchestration-centric methods, adapting decisions dynamically while leaving coordination structure implicit and unstable. To address this challenge, we revisit multi-agent coordination from a probabilistic perspective, casting it as posterior inference over the joint distribution of structure and orchestration. We introduce MACA, an automated coordination framework that learns a task- and budget-conditioned structural prior over agent participation and interactions. This prior guides a policy-based orchestration as an approximation to posterior inference, enabling efficient solutions with fine-grained control. Across benchmarks, MACA outperforms adaptive multi-agent baselines by an average of 8.42% while using 43.19% fewer tokens. Further investigation reveals that joint adaptation of structure and orchestration suppresses redundant interactions, converging coordination toward task-effective execution.
2605.25746.paper_summary_zh: 隨著基於大型語言模型（LLM）的多代理系統擴展以處理日益複雜的任務，平衡結構穩定性和動態適應性變得越來越具有挑戰性。現有系統通常採用結構中心的方法，承諾使用事先確定的結構，這限制了細粒度的控制，或採用協調中心的方法，動態調整決策，同時使協調結構隱性且不穩定。為了解決這一挑戰，我們從概率的角度重新審視多代理協調，將其視為對結構和協調的聯合分佈進行後驗推斷。我們引入了MACA，一個自動化的協調框架，該框架學習基於任務和預算的代理參與和互動的結構先驗。這一先驗指導基於策略的協調，作為後驗推斷的近似，實現高效的解決方案並提供細粒度的控制。在基準測試中，MACA的表現超過了自適應多代理基線，平均提升了8.42%，同時使用了43.19%更少的標記。進一步的調查顯示，結構和協調的聯合適應抑制了冗餘的互動，將協調收斂到任務有效的執行。
2605.25746.author: Haoran Li et.al.
2605.25746.authors: Haoran Li, Shulun Chen, Shaoyuan Sun, Hanchen Wang
